xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: "Saket Callipolis", Unit No. 601 & 602, 6th Floor, Doddakannalli Village, Varthur Hobli, Sarjapur Road, Bangalore 560035
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: "Saket Callipolis", Unit No. 601 & 602, 6th Floor, Doddakannalli Village, Varthur Hobli, Sarjapur Road, Bangalore 560035
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan:
Barbeque Nation, a leading name in the casual dining industry, is celebrated for its diverse and flavourful cuisine. Our restaurant is built on the foundation of providing a memorable and interactive dining experience, where guests can enjoy the unique "over-the-table barbeque" concept, allowing them to grill their own appetizers right at their table. With an extensive all-you-can-eat menu featuring a variety of vegetarian and non-vegetarian delicacies, we ensure that every visit is a culinary indulgence that caters to a wide range of tastes and preferences.
Our welcoming ambiance and commitment to quality make Barbeque Nation a preferred destination for all kinds of gatherings. Whether it’s families looking to bond over a delicious meal, corporate teams strengthening their camaraderie, or friends celebrating special occasions, our restaurant provides the perfect setting for every moment.
Beyond just offering a dining experience, Barbeque Nation is synonymous with celebrations. We take pride in being a part of our guests' festivities, curating themed menus and immersive dining experiences for various occasions. From lively New Year parties to joyous Holi feasts, from grand Ramadan spreads to illuminating Diwali celebrations, our restaurants transform into vibrant hubs of festivity, ensuring that every occasion is celebrated with warmth, delicious food, and an unforgettable atmosphere.
At Barbeque Nation, we go beyond serving food, we create lasting memories, bringing people together over great flavours and cherished moments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Barbeque Nation MENA Holding Limited, Dubai
Red Apple Kitchen Consultancy Private Limited, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Strict regulatory requirements and guest expectations necessitate adherence to food safety standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Continuous quality audits, staff training, and investment in high-standard storage & preparation processes
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Reducing energy consumption in our kitchens and restaurants lowers operational costs and environmental footprint.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementing energy-efficient appliances, optimizing cooking processes, and utilizing renewable energy where feasible.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Employee satisfaction and retention directly impact service quality and operational efficiency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Competitive wages, training programs, career development initiatives, and wellness programs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Extreme weather events can disrupt ingredient supply chains, impacting costs and availability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Developing diversified sourcing strategies, strengthening supplier contracts, and investing in climate-resilient logistics
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ensuring a reliable and ethical supply chain enhances brand reputation and guest trust
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Strengthening partnerships with responsible suppliers, implementing traceability measures, and prioritizing locally sourced ingredients.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: All our policies have been formulated in accordance with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct, 2019, issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs.
All our policies have been formulated in accordance with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct, 2019, issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs.

All our policies have been formulated in accordance with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct, 2019, issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs.
All our policies have been formulated in accordance with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct, 2019, issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs.

All our policies have been formulated in accordance with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct, 2019, issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: At Barbeque Nation, we have set clear goals and targets to emphasize environmental protection, employee well-being, and guest safety. Our strategies and operations are designed to uphold these commitments, fostering a sustainable and secure environment for everyone.

At Barbeque Nation, we have set clear goals and targets to emphasize environmental protection, employee well-being, and guest safety. Our strategies and operations are designed to uphold these commitments, fostering a sustainable and secure environment for everyone.

At Barbeque Nation, we have set clear goals and targets to emphasize environmental protection, employee well-being, and guest safety. Our strategies and operations are designed to uphold these commitments, fostering a sustainable and secure environment for everyone.

At Barbeque Nation, we have set clear goals and targets to emphasize environmental protection, employee well-being, and guest safety. Our strategies and operations are designed to uphold these commitments, fostering a sustainable and secure environment for everyone.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: The report begins with a message from our Managing Director, providing key insights and strategic direction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: The Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee (CSRS) of the Board of Directors is responsible for overseeing the implementation of Business Responsibility Policies and making key decisions on sustainability matters. This committee ensures compliance with these policies while driving sustainability initiatives. As of March 31, 2025, the committee comprises the following members: • Mr. Abhay Chaudhari – Non-Executive, Independent Director (Member) • Mr. Kayum Dhanani – Managing Director (Member) • Mrs. Suchitra Dhanani – Non-Executive Director (Member)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee (CSRS) of the Board of Directors is responsible for overseeing the implementation of Business Responsibility Policies and making key decisions on sustainability matters. This committee ensures compliance with these policies while driving sustainability initiatives. As of March 31, 2025, the committee comprises the following members: • Mr. Abhay Chaudhari – Non-Executive, Independent Director (Member) • Mr. Kayum Dhanani – Managing Director (Member) • Mrs. Suchitra Dhanani – Non-Executive Director (Member)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfOtherCommitteeFor: The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.

The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.

The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.

The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.

The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.

The review has been conducted by both the Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability Committee and the CEO. They are responsible for overseeing and evaluating these processes to ensure alignment with our sustainability objectives.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Employees have been given training on all principles Principle 1 and other Principles as applicable to their respective functional area
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: The Code of Conduct for the Board of Directors & Senior Management comprehensively addresses concerns regarding anti-corruption and anti-bribery policies. These policies are accessible at https://www.barbequenation.com/corporategovernance-policies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: We have implemented a comprehensive Code of Conduct for its directors and senior management, which mandates that all individuals act in the best interest of the organization. This code emphasizes the importance of ensuring that no personal or professional associations create a conflict of interest with the Company’s operations. To uphold these standards, directors and senior management are required to carefully assess their external engagements and activities to avoid any real or perceived conflicts. If any actual or potential conflict of interest arises, the concerned director is obligated to promptly disclose the matter to the Board of Directors. This disclosure allows for an appropriate review and resolution, ensuring that the integrity and transparency of the Company’s governance processes are maintained. Through this proactive approach, we reinforces our commitment to ethical practices and robust corporate governance
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron:
For the financial year 2024-25, the following eco-friendly measures have been incorporated in all new restaurants opened during the year with a capex of Rs. 74,94,087 Smoke eliminators have been installed to reduce CO level inside the restaurants in addition to air scrubbers that cleans the air before releasing it in the environment.
Grease traps in conjunction with ETP/STPs ensures efficient and effective wastewater treatment before discharging.
IOT Based Electricity management: Use of Variable Frequency Device (VFD) panels that automatically detects power requirements and adjust all equipment operations to save power.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableCondu: Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText: Not Applicable considering the nature of the business
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Employee Helpdesk: Every employee within the organization has the opportunity to address their daily concerns through a mobile application to the centralized HR Shared Services (HRSS) team. The HRSS team is responsible for promptly resolving these grievances within a 24-hour timeframe.
Employee Happiness Index: At our organization, we firmly believe that happy employees lead to satisfied guests. To gauge the happiness and satisfaction levels of our employees, we conduct quarterly surveys. These surveys involve a comprehensive questionnaire designed to gather feedback on various aspects of their work life, organizational culture, and physical amenities. The findings from these surveys, along with areas of improvement identified, are shared with the respective Business Manager and Cluster Manager for resolution. Additionally, the survey scores have a direct impact on the Balance Score Card of stakeholders.
Open House: On a monthly basis, the designated HRBP (Human Resource Business Partner) visits our restaurants to conduct Open House Sessions with all employees working in each location. During these sessions, employees have the opportunity to voice any specific grievances they may have directly to the HRBP
Voice of Employee (VOE): Our organization provides a platform known as Voice of Employee (VOE), enabling employees to report any instances of illegal, dishonest, or fraudulent activity or misconduct directly to the Core Value Committee. Upon receipt of a reported complaint, the Core Value Committee conducts a thorough investigation and takes appropriate actions as necessary.
SKIP Session: Periodically, the respective Cluster Manager conducts SKIP sessions with outlet employees to address and resolve any specific issues they may be facing. During these sessions, employees have the opportunity to share their concerns, and the Cluster Manager works towards finding suitable solutions. On Periodical basis, Respective Cluster Manager conducts SKIP session with outlet employees to understand and solve any specific issues

Employee Happiness Index: At our organization, we firmly believe that happy employees lead to satisfied guests. To gauge the happiness and satisfaction levels of our employees, we conduct quarterly surveys. These surveys involve a comprehensive questionnaire designed to gather feedback on various aspects of their work life, organizational culture, and physical amenities. The findings from these surveys, along with areas of improvement identified, are shared with the respective Business Manager and Cluster Manager for resolution. Additionally, the survey scores have a direct impact on the Balance Score Card of stakeholders.
Open House: On a monthly basis, the designated HRBP (Human Resource Business Partner) visits our restaurants to conduct Open House Sessions with all employees working in each location. During these sessions, employees have the opportunity to voice any specific grievances they may have directly to the HRBP
Voice of Employee (VOE): Our organization provides a platform known as Voice of Employee (VOE), enabling employees to report any instances of illegal, dishonest, or fraudulent activity or misconduct directly to the Core Value Committee. Upon receipt of a reported complaint, the Core Value Committee conducts a thorough investigation and takes appropriate actions as necessary.
SKIP Session: Periodically, the respective Cluster Manager conducts SKIP sessions with outlet employees to address and resolve any specific issues they may be facing. During these sessions, employees have the opportunity to share their concerns, and the Cluster Manager works towards finding suitable solutions. On Periodical basis, Respective Cluster Manager conducts SKIP session with outlet employees to understand and solve any specific issues
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, we are dedicated to establishing and preserving a secure and healthy work environment through the establishment and maintenance of a robust health and safety management system. Our Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS) policy offers directives and measures for ensuring the well- being of all individuals within the Barbeque Nation environment, including employees, visitors, vendors, guests, and contractors. This policy aims to prevent any activities undertaken by employees from endangering the health and safety of themselves or others. The objectives of our effectively implemented health and safety management system include: • Comply with all Health & Safety Legislation, Regulations, Codes of Practice, and Guidance Notes. Provide and maintain safe and healthy working conditions for all employees and those working on our behalf. • Identify risks and, if elimination is not possible, control them to an acceptable level. • Value the contributions of all employees in the realm of Health & Safety equally with their other contributions to the business. • Require every employee and those working on our behalf to take personal responsibility in preventing harm to themselves and others. • Actively work to prevent all workplace accidents and near-miss incidents. • Encourage the open reporting of all accidents and near-miss incidents and their investigation to prevent recurrence. • Provide necessary information, instruction, and training to enable employees to fully cooperate with us in achieving compliance with the policy. • Routinely monitor and review EHS performance to achieve continuous improvement. • Set Health & Safety targets and maintain an improvement plan to address areas needing improvement. • The occupational health and safety management system covers all restaurants and offices. The Cluster Manager oversees the implementation of EHS Standards at all restaurants, with support from the entire Outlet team. The Cluster Manager conducts monthly reviews of EHS status and documents the proceedings.The responsibility of the Business Manager in implementing EHS Standards at the outlet includes: • Cultivating a “Safe Work Culture” • Conducting induction sessions for new employees on Health and Safety Practices Organizing meetings, trainings, and awareness campaigns. • Ensuring effective EHS inspections, adherence to rules and procedures, and implementation of permit systems. • Fostering continuous awareness through motivational talks, safety meetings, and slogans. • Maintaining effective housekeeping. • Keeping records of EHS compliance for all statutory requirements
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The EHS policy outlines processes to systematically identify work-related hazards and assess risks on both routine and non-routine bases.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: We have implemented a strong EHS management system with clearly defined roles and responsibilities to ensure effective execution of safety measures. Our Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Policy is designed to foster a safe and respectful work environment, free from all forms of harassment, and to safeguard employees from sexual harassment. Under the PoSH policy, a grievance redressal mechanism is in place to provide an efficient process for addressing and resolving any instances of harassment, including sexual harassment, ensuring the well-being of all employees. Details regarding the grievance redressal mechanism for addressing various concerns are outlined in point 6.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: We implement various measures to guarantee the safety and well-being of both our guests and staff members. Regular audits and safety inspections are carried out to maintain the seamless and secure functioning of our operations. Our team undergoes frequent training sessions on fire safety and emergency evacuation protocols to effectively manage any potential emergencies, ensuring the safe evacuation of individuals with diverse abilities. Additionally, we prioritize continuous improvement in our safety practices to uphold the highest standards of security for all stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: We promote compliance by fostering long-term relationships with reputable and certified suppliers. We also prefer sourcing from partners who comply with industry certifications such as HACCP and BAP, which often include regulatory compliance as part of their requirements. This structured approach indirectly supports the alignment of suppliers with statutory and quality obligations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: At present, there is no formal documentation or disclosure of specific corrective actions taken as a result of assessments conducted on value chain partners’ health and safety practices. However, the entity continues to strengthen its engagement through informal monitoring, feedback mechanisms, and certification requirements. Vendors supplying food ingredients are often selected based on adherence to safety protocols and compliance certifications, which reduces the need for downstream corrective actions. Should any non-compliance arise, it is expected that such issues would be addressed promptly through internal review and partner communication channels.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Stakeholders encompass individuals or groups whose interests are influenced or may potentially be influenced by an organization’s actions. Our identification of key stakeholders, both internal and external, is based on their direct impact on our operations and where our business can make the most significant difference. Barbeque-Nation Hospitality Limited acknowledges its stakeholders to include current and prospective guests, employees, shareholders, investors, regulatory bodies, media outlets, and the community.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: The organization does not indicate a direct mechanism by which stakeholder consultations on economic, environmental, or social topics are escalated to the Board. However, Barbeque Nation has established dedicated committees, such as the CSR and Sustainability Committee and the Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee, which oversee matters of public concern and stakeholder expectations. These committees likely serve as the medium through which functional insights gathered from guest feedback, employee surveys, community outreach, and supplier reviews are consolidated and reflected in strategic decisions. This delegation framework allows for relevant stakeholder feedback to inform Board-level discussions, albeit indirectly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Yes, stakeholder consultations are used to inform the identification and management of environmental and social topics. Barbeque Nation engages various stakeholders on an ongoing basis. Feedback from guests is gathered through tools like the Guest Satisfaction Index, while employee perspectives are incorporated through quarterly happiness surveys and regular HRBP interactions. These insights contribute to decisions related to, safety protocols, and gender-inclusive practices. Similarly, engagements with NGOs and community stakeholders under CSR initiatives provide input that helps shape future campaigns and social interventions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: The Company demonstrates a strong commitment to engaging with and supporting marginalized stakeholder groups through inclusive initiatives. The CSR program Iftar Fest serves meals to underprivileged children across cities in India addressing food insecurity. Employment-linked skill development through PMKVY has enabled 3,000 rural youth to gain training and provided job opportunities to 1,000 of them. Internally, the Company launched initiatives like Barbeque Rani and Aapki Saheli to create safer and more empowering work environments for women. These examples underscore how the Company is integrating equity and inclusion into both its social responsibility framework and its internal culture.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Guest Feedback/Guest Satisfaction Index (GSI) for each outlet, loyalty program (SMILES), Website, Social Media Platforms
Notice Boards, Website, Employee Survey feedback, Annual Performance Review, Meetings, Trainings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Working condition, Employee performance, Employee Satisfaction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: AGM, Investor meets, Investor Grievance redressal mechanism
Press Releases, Social Media Platforms, Media interactions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Internal mechanisms have been established to address grievances concerning human rights matters. The POSH Committee is designated to handle complaints related to sexual harassment. For other human rights concerns, individuals can approach the ‘Core Value Committee’ directly, where the Chief People Officer collaborates to resolve the issue effectively
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: The Whistle Blower Policy, Sexual harassment Policy provides the mechanism to prevent adverse consequences to the complainant in discrimination and harassment cases.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: During the reporting period, Barbeque Nation did not record any major human rights grievances that required a change in business processes. However, the Company has proactively strengthened its internal mechanisms to pre-empt such issues. The introduction of the Barbeque Rani mascot and the Aapki Saheli initiative reflect a commitment to building safer and more inclusive environments for women employees. These initiatives go beyond compliance by promoting gendersensitive communication, open discussion forums, and sensitization sessions for male employees. While these programs were not launched as a direct response to grievances, they represent a proactive modification in internal communication, learning, and culture-building processes aimed at upholding human rights in the workplace.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: The Company has not conducted a standalone human rights due diligence as per the formal definition involving third party audits or deep value chain assessments. However, Barbeque Nation embeds key components of human rights protection through various internal systems. These include the implementation of Equal Opportunity and Anti-Sexual Harassment policies, mandatory PoSH training for all categories of employees, a centralized grievance redressal mechanism, and a Core Value Committee that handles misconduct cases. All 100% of employees permanent and contractual have received training on sexual harassment awareness. Furthermore, safety and ethical practices are integral to the supplier code of conduct. These practices collectively offer partial coverage of human rights due diligence, focused primarily on internal operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc:
We are strategically adopting and executing effective energy conservation measures at every feasible juncture. These efforts are efficiently directed towards conserving energy consumption across all our restaurants and office facilities. Key initiatives undertaken by the Company to promote energy conservation and reduce greenhouse gas emissions include: a. Implementation of LED lighting systems to minimize energy consumption during operations. Deployment of an IoT-based Electricity Management system, featuring Variable Frequency Device (VFD) panels in new restaurants. These panels automatically detect power requirements and optimize equipment operations to conserve power.
b.Ongoing efforts to implement an energy management system for AC units and refrigerators to monitor and optimize energy usage.
c. Adoption of briquette coal, which emits lower carbon compared to wood charcoal.
d. Introduction of an IoT-based Electricity Management system, integrating VFD panels in new restaurants to optimize power consumption.
e. Phasing out single-use plastic bottles across all restaurants, resulting in an anticipated annual reduction of approximately 100 tonnes of plastic waste.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Waste segregation, encompassing both wet and dry waste, is a compulsory procedure practiced across all restaurants. It is integrated into our routine employee training programs. All waste materials are directed towards recycling through approved collectors. Regarding e-waste, we prioritize asset reuse, and when items reach the end of their lifecycle, they are handed over to authorized recyclers or collectors. Additionally, batteries and used oil are disposed of responsibly through approved vendors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: Yes, the Company has a Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCDR) plan in place, overseen by a cross-functional committee. This framework ensures that restaurant operations can quickly adapt to disruptions such as fire, natural calamities, cyberattacks, or health emergencies like pandemics. The plan includes SOPs for food safety, employee evacuation, backup utility systems, data recovery, and emergency supplier coordination.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: While the core operations of Barbeque Nation have a relatively low direct environmental impact, the value chain particularly in food production and logistics, carries a more substantial footprint. The Company addresses this by sourcing from environmentally certified processors, encouraging local procurement to reduce transportation emissions, and minimizing packaging waste. Waste segregation at source and responsible disposal practices are implemented in collaboration with vendors. As the environmental disclosure framework matures, the Company is committed to developing vendor engagement programs that align with sustainable supply chain goals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Use of forecasting tools to estimate food preparation during a day based on the reservation and past trends.
We use RSPO-certified cooking oil, and responsibly recycle 100% of used cooking oil into biofuels, reinforcing our sustainable future commitment
Use of LED lighting, energy-efficient kitchen appliances across all restaurants
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: There are several mechanisms in place to receive grievances from the community such as access to Website, guest help-line/Toll-free number, Barbeque Nation App, and every outlet. The grievances from community are addressed by Legal and Compliance officer along-with Stakeholders’ Relationship Management Committee of the Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: The entity sources goods and services, such as local produce, cleaning supplies, and catering-related items, from self-help groups (SHGs), women-led cooperatives, and minority community enterprises, especially in Tier 2/3 cities where vendor diversity is promoted.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: To ensure effective communication with consumers, we’ve established multiple avenues for feedback and complaint resolution. These include a dedicated guest helpline number and toll-free service, as well as a user-friendly interface on our website. Additionally, we conduct regular surveys through our Guest Satisfaction Index, reaching out to guests daily to gather their insights. Moreover, we take a proactive approach to address consumer concerns by promptly sharing actionable feedback with outlet managers. This ensures that any issues raised are swiftly addressed and corrective measures are implemented for continuous improvement. By fostering open communication channels, we aim to enhance guest satisfaction and strengthen our relationship with consumers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsForVoluntaryRecall: We take great efforts to serve the food with best quality ingredients and the best of the preparation methods. We have no instances of any product recall.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsForForcedRecall: We take great efforts to serve the food with best quality ingredients and the best of the preparation methods. We have no instances of any product recall.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No regulatory actions have been taken concerning advertising, essential services, cyber security, data privacy, or product recalls in our Company's history.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Barbeque Nation provides comprehensive information about its products, services, offers, and guest experiences through multiple digital and offline platforms. The primary digital channel is its official website: https://www.barbequenation.com, where guests can view menus, book tables, explore special campaigns (e.g., seasonal festivals), and access contact details. The brand also maintains a strong presence on social media platforms like Instagram, Facebook, and Twitter to engage with guests, share updates, and promote events. In addition, information is accessible through the Barbeque Nation mobile app available on Android and iOS, as well as thirdparty food delivery apps such as Zomato and Swiggy
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: While the Company operates in the dine-in and food service sector where traditional product usage instructions may not apply it still emphasizes guest safety and responsible dining. Measures include in-restaurant hygiene guidelines, clearly marked allergen information (where applicable), and employee training on food safety
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: Barbeque Nation ensures transparent communication with its guests regarding operational changes, disruptions, or discontinuations. If any outlet experiences temporary closure or a shift in service availability (e.g., dine-in to delivery only), updates are promptly communicated through SMS notifications, in-app alerts, Google business updates, and guest care helplines. During public health emergencies or lockdowns, the Company adapts quickly to government directives and ensures that guests are informed about service status, new safety norms, and available alternatives such as takeaway or cloud kitchens
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProductInformationOnT: Yes, Barbeque Nation displays product and service information that often exceeds statutory requirements. While food labeling laws are limited in the dine-in segment, the Company provides allergen advice, information on vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes, and calorieconscious options in select campaigns. Interactive menus and chef counters allow guests to inquire about food content and customize meals based on dietary preferences. Yes, the entity regularly conducts guest satisfaction surveys through digital feedback forms, the Guest Satisfaction Index, and in-restaurant feedback tablets. Insights from these surveys are used to improve food quality, staff behavior, service speed, and overall guest experience. This consumer-first approach is a key part of Barbeque Nation’s service model
